_comment: nrxr — backend↔frontend getModelFamily() parity fixture. Loaded by BOTH backend/internal/analytics/pricing_test.go and frontend/src/utils/tokenStats.test.ts so the two normalizers can never silently drift. Each entry pins the family key each side must return for `id`: `backend` = getModelFamily(id) (provider-agnostic, sniffs gpt- prefix), `frontend` = getModelFamily(provider, id). They are equal for every well-formed id; the one deliberate divergence (the malformed claude-opus-4a-5) is encoded as intentional and carries a `note`. Add a row whenever a new family/shape ships; if a code change makes a row's two sides disagree unexpectedly, that's the drift this guard exists to catch.

cases.note: MALFORMED id (non-digit major '4a'). Documented, intentional divergence: backend's single-digit-major check fails and returns the raw name 'opus-4a-5' ($0 — no pricing match); the frontend regex stops at 'a' and returns 'opus-4' (which could bind to real opus-4 pricing). Low severity — such ids don't occur in practice. This row pins the KNOWN divergence so any further drift fails CI; do not 'fix' it by editing the fixture (5x6e F7 / nrxr).
